text: Aim and scope: The aim of this study was to determine how people in the middle age in Poland understand agritourism and to assess their attitudes towards it, and also to identify the behavior of rural tourists and their expectations in terms of rural tourism activities. Based on the results of a diagnostic survey in which 326 respondents took part (in the age range from 35 to 65), in this study we analyzed the motivations for choosing agritourism (mainly related to health, peacefulness, relaxation), factors influencing the choice of particular farms (principally the price standard level and availability of local food), sources of information, tourist preferences regarding outdoor leisure activities, expectations regarding possible options for improving the current offer on the agritourism market and thereby satisfying the emerging demands of these customers. Conclusion: The results show that in order to increase agritourism as a significant alternative for developing rural tourism, it is necessary to improve the image of this type of tourism and, as a result, to increase the interest of potential customers, and therefore also to promote the quality of agricultural tourism infrastructure.
